channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] E.U. Orders Google to Open Android Mic, Camera and Screen to Rival AI Assistants
item.description: The European Commission on Thursday ordered Google to give rival AI assistants the same reach into Android that Gemini already has: the camera, the microphone, whatever is on screen, a wake word that fires with the…

item.title: [Critique] The Race to Field Military Autonomy Is On, Can Trusted Information Infrastructure Keep Pace?
item.description: Military forces are under increasing pressure to field autonomous capabilities faster than ever before. Across the U.S., UK, and NATO, new investment, evolving defense strategies, and accelerated acquisition pathways…

item.title: [Critique] New Windows LegacyHive zero-day gives hackers admin privileges
item.description: A security researcher using the "Nightmare Eclipse" handle has released a Windows zero-day exploit dubbed LegacyHive that allows attackers to escalate privileges on up-to-date Windows systems. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Armenia Detains Russian Tourist on U.S. Warrant for REvil Hacker, Lawyers Say Wrong Man
item.description:
Armenia has held a Russian tourist named Aleksandr Ermakov in a detention center since June 28, on a U.S. extradition request for a REvil ransomware suspect named Aleksandr Ermakov.
His wife, Maria Yurova, told REN TV…

item.title: [Faible] 16 millions de CV exposés en ligne
item.description: Une annonce pirate revendique 16 millions de CV, avec coordonnées, adresses et historiques professionnels.

item.title: [Faible] Windows Server 2022 reach end of mainstream support in 90 days
item.description: Microsoft announced that Windows Server 2022 will reach the mainstream end date in October 2026, but will switch to extended support and continue receiving security updates for five more years. [...]

item.title: [Faible] ACR Stealer Uses ClickFix Lures to Steal Browser Tokens and Microsoft 365 Files
item.description: ACR Stealer, an infostealer in circulation since 2024, is walking out of enterprise networks with saved browser passwords, live session tokens, PDFs, Microsoft 365 documents, and files from synced OneDrive and…

item.title: [Faible] New GoSerpent Malware Targets Southeast Asian Governments and Diplomats for Espionage
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented malware called GoSerpent that has been put to use in cyber attacks targeting entities in Southeast Asia since late 2025 with a focus on long-term…

item.title: [Faible] US charges two over laundering $43 million from investment fraud
item.description: U.S. prosecutors on Thursday charged a New York man and woman for their roles in a large-scale crime ring that laundered money stolen in cyber investment fraud scams. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] CISA urges immediate action on actively exploited Fortinet flaws
item.description: CISA on Thursday ordered government agencies to prioritize patching two actively exploited vulnerabilities in the Fortinet FortiSandbox threat detection platform. [...]

item.title: [Critique] CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Thursday added a newly patched security flaw impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, requiring…

item.title: [Élevé] Europ-Camera informe ses clients d’une fuite de données après une cyberattaque
item.description: Le spécialiste français de la vidéosurveillance et des systèmes de sécurité Europ-Camera a informé une partie de ses... L’article Europ-Camera informe ses clients d’une fuite de données après une cyberattaque est apparu…

item.title: [Critique] New ClickLock macOS malware traps users into revealing login password
item.description: A new macOS information-stealing malware dubbed ClickLock terminates all visible processes to force users into entering their system login password. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Coca-Cola says Fairlife ransomware attack halts US dairy production
item.description: The Coca-Cola Company disclosed today that a ransomware attack impacting its Fairlife dairy subsidiary has disrupted operations, temporarily suspending production of Fairlife products across the United States. [...]

item.title: [Faible] Senator calls on Rubio, Blanche to push back against Canadian surveillance legislation
item.description: Democratic Sen. Ron Wyden says the Trump administration should pressure Canada not to enact a proposal that would "weaponize American technology infrastructure" for surveillance purposes.

item.title: [Critique] Claude Chrome extension flaw lets malicious extensions trigger AI actions
item.description: A flaw in Anthropic's Claude for Chrome browser extension could allow a malicious extension to trigger predefined AI actions by simulating user clicks, potentially allowing it to abuse Claude's access to connected…

item.title: [Élevé] Actiale : 229 978 missions en fuite après une cyberattaque
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Actiale a été revendiquée le 15 juillet 2026. Le dépôt annoncé contiendrait... L’article Actiale : 229 978 missions en fuite après une cyberattaque est apparu en premier sur…

item.title: [Élevé] Croq’Vacances : 72 916 personnes et 24 Go de documents dans une fuite massive
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Croq’Vacances a été revendiquée le 15 juillet 2026. L’organisation française de colonies... L’article Croq’Vacances : 72 916 personnes et 24 Go de documents dans une fuite massive est…

item.title: [Élevé] Centres Sociaux de France : les données revendiqués après une compromission de RezoFed
item.description: Une compromission attribuée à RezoFed, la plateforme interne de la Fédération des Centres Sociaux et Socioculturels de France... L’article Centres Sociaux de France : les données revendiqués après une compromission de…

item.title: [Faible] New OkoBot framework deploys 20 payloads to steal data, crypto
item.description: A new malicious framework called OkoBot is delivering more than 20 payloads in attacks focused on stealing cryptocurrency wallet seed phrases, credentials, and other sensitive data. [...]

item.title: [Élevé] EVA.gg : 70 424 comptes et 119 361 réservations revendiqués après l’exploitation de son API GraphQL
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à EVA.gg a été revendiquée le 16 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir récupéré... L’article EVA.gg : 70 424 comptes et 119 361 réservations revendiqués après l’exploitation de son API…

item.title: [Élevé] Accor : 162 437 fiches clients revendiquées après l’accès à un outil interne du groupe
item.description: Une fuite de données attribuée à Accor a été revendiquée le 16 juillet 2026. L’auteur affirme avoir compromis... L’article Accor : 162 437 fiches clients revendiquées après l’accès à un outil interne du groupe est…

item.title: [Élevé] Sapeurs Pompiers (FNSPF) : 124 807 pompiers dans une fuite de données
item.description: Une double revendication vise Pompiers.fr et la Fédération nationale des sapeurs-pompiers de France (FNSPF). Les publications annoncent la... L’article Sapeurs Pompiers (FNSPF) : 124 807 pompiers dans une fuite de…

item.title: [Critique] Two Scattered Spider Hackers Get 5.5 Years Each for £29 Million TfL Hack
item.description:
Owen Flowers, 18, and Thalha Jubair, 20, were each sentenced to five and a half years at Woolwich Crown Court on Thursday, 16 July 2026, for the 2024 hack of Transport for London.
The attack left 148 TfL systems…

item.title: [Faible] UK investigates TikTok for alleged age-verification lapses, exposing kids to online harms
item.description: “Age checks are a cornerstone of the UK’s online safety laws,” said Ofcom’s Chief Executive, Melanie Dawes. “Too many services have no or inadequate age checks in place, which is not good enough.”

item.title: [Faible] Ukrainians rally against dismissal of tech-minded defense minister Fedorov
item.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky dismissed Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov, who championed the push to integrate drone technology and digital innovation into the military.

item.title: [Critique] ThreatsDay: Game Cheat Spyware, 24-Hour Ransomware, Chrome Sync Stalking + 12 More Stories
item.description:
A lot of this week’s trouble starts with something that looks close enough.
A familiar repo. A useful installer. A harmless sync setting. Then the handoff goes bad, the box starts talking to someone else, and the…

item.title: [Faible] AI Agents Broke the Security Playbook. Here's What Replaces It.
item.description: Traditional security workflows were built for environments that changed at human speed. Token Security explains why AI agents require a new approach: building on a live identity foundation while giving security teams…

item.title: [Faible] Sandworm hackers have a CAPTCHA trick for Ukrainians
item.description: Rather than verifying they are human, the CAPTCHA users are instructed to copy and paste a PowerShell command into their Windows computers.

item.title: [Élevé] 23andMe to pay $18 million in new genetics data breach settlement
item.description: Genetic testing company 23andMe has agreed to pay $18 million to settle claims from a coalition of 43 attorneys general that it failed to protect customers' genetic data. [...]

item.title: [Faible] n8n Token Exchange Flaw Could Let Attackers Log In as Users From Another Issuer
item.description: n8n, the workflow automation platform, handed out the wrong accounts at login. On Enterprise instances configured to trust more than one external token issuer, it matched an incoming JWT to a local user on…

item.title: [Faible] New TELEPUZ Malware Spreads via ClickFix to Steal Data and Run Commands
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have called attention to a new modular malware called TELEPUZ that's been spreading via websites infected with ClickFix lures since late April 2026.
"The malware is full-featured, lightweight,…

item.title: [Faible] New ClickLock macOS Stealer Kills Apps Every 210ms Until Victims Type Their Password
item.description: ClickLock Stealer, a new macOS infostealer, answers a victim's refusal by killing their apps on a loop until they hand over the login password. It arrives as a command pasted into Terminal, asks for the password behind…

item.title: [Faible] Scattered Spider members behind TfL hack get five years in prison
item.description: Two leading members of the Scattered Spider cybercrime collective were sentenced to five years and six months in prison each for hacking Transport for London (TfL) in 2024. [...]
